items.title: HCL Technologies acquires 10.46% stake in Sarvam AI for ₹1,427 crore
items.summary: HCL Technologies acquired a 10.46% minority stake in Axonwise Private Limited (operator of Sarvam AI) for ₹1,427.25 crore, making it one of the most significant AI investment moves by a large-cap Indian IT firm. Sarvam AI is known for its India-specific AI models including multilingual LLMs.
items.rationale: HCLTech's strategic AI acquisition signals a decisive pivot to native AI capabilities, differentiating it from peers still reliant on third-party AI layers. A minority stake gives HCLTech access to Sarvam's India-specific language models while managing capital risk. Positive for re-rating vs IT peers.

items.title: GIC OFS: Government to sell up to 5% stake; floor price ₹352/share — opens June 16-17
items.summary: Government of India is selling up to 5% equity in General Insurance Corporation via OFS at floor price ₹352/share; base offer 2%, with option for additional 3%. Non-retail investors on June 16, retail on June 17. Proceeds go towards government's FY27 divestment programme.
items.rationale: OFS announcements create near-term supply overhang and anchor price at or near the floor. Short-term pressure on GIC Re shares, though strong institutional interest and relative undervaluation may absorb the supply quickly.

items.title: Tata Motors falls 5% as JLR flags near-zero free cash flow in FY26, macro risks
items.summary: Tata Motors shares slipped over 5% on June 16 after JLR, its UK luxury arm, flagged 'close to zero' free cash flow expectations for FY26 alongside macro risks in its investor presentation. JLR reaffirmed EBIT margin guidance of 5-7% for FY26.
items.rationale: Near-zero FCF guidance signals JLR's balance sheet stress; combined with macro uncertainties (UK economy, US tariffs on autos), this materially limits Tata Motors' dividend and deleveraging capacity. Near-term earnings revisions likely to be negative.

items.title: SBI board to consider FY27 fundraise on June 18 — public offer or private placement
items.summary: State Bank of India's board will meet on June 18 to consider raising funds in FY27 through a public offer or private placement. The fundraise is part of SBI's capital augmentation plan to support loan book growth.
items.rationale: Capital raising is a double-edged catalyst — it signals growth ambitions and regulatory capital headroom but may be dilutive to existing shareholders. Watch for size and structure of the fundraise for directional read.

items.title: Craftsman Automation launches ₹2,000 crore QIP at floor price ₹8,966/share
items.summary: Craftsman Automation has launched a ₹2,000 crore QIP with floor price ₹8,966.13 per share. The proceeds are expected to fund capex and debt repayment. Craftsman is an auto-component and industrial manufacturer with diversified end-market exposure.
items.rationale: QIP dilution may weigh near-term on per-share metrics but signals management confidence in growth visibility. Institutional participation at the QIP price will be the key valuation benchmark.

items.title: Sensex jumps 736 pts, Nifty closes above 23,854 on US-Iran peace deal announcement
items.summary: Indian benchmarks rallied sharply on June 15: Sensex +736 pts to 76,264 and Nifty +231 pts to 23,853.90. Nifty briefly touched 24,000 intraday — a key resistance level. Realty index outperformed (+~4%). Pharma and healthcare were the only sectors in red. FIIs turned net buyers (+₹200 Cr) for the first time in weeks.
items.rationale: Peace deal removes the geopolitical risk premium; lower crude directly benefits India's CAD, inflation, and rate-sensitive sectors. FII reversal to net buying is a critical sentiment signal if it sustains.

items.title: India-US trade talks to resume June 23-24; USTR Greer to visit India on June 22
items.summary: USTR Jamieson Greer will visit New Delhi on June 22, with talks on June 23-24 to finalise the India-US interim trade agreement (BTA). Commerce Secretary said 'final touches' are being given to the framework. Commerce Minister Goyal and US Ambassador Gor both described 99% of issues as resolved. An interim deal is expected by mid-July 2026.
items.rationale: Finalising the India-US BTA removes the Section 301 tariff overhang for IT services, pharma generics, textiles and auto parts. Removes a key FII selling trigger and provides fresh earnings visibility for export-oriented sectors.

items.title: Brent crude plunges over 4% to ~$83.34/bbl as US-Iran sign ceasefire; rebounds to $83.24 on June 16
items.summary: Brent crude fell over 4% to ~$83.34/bbl on June 15 after US-Iran preliminary peace deal. On June 16, crude rebounded 0.3% to ~$83.24/bbl as markets assessed uncertainty over the pace of Strait of Hormuz reopening. Brent had traded above $124/bbl at the West Asia conflict peak.
items.rationale: Sharp crude decline is directly bearish for upstream E&P realisations (ONGC, OIL India). OMC marketing margins are complex — improved if retail prices are not cut. The June 16 partial rebound signals crude supply restoration may take longer, providing modest near-term support to upstream names.

items.title: India's WPI inflation surges to 9.68% in May on Fuel & Power jump of 30.33%
items.summary: India's WPI inflation rose to 9.68% in May from 8.26% in April. Fuel and Power category surged 30.33% YoY; mineral oils +49.82%; crude petroleum +61.51%. Manufactured products rose 7.48%. First reading under revised WPI 2022-23 base year series.
items.rationale: High WPI signals elevated input cost pressure for manufacturing. However, the May data reflects war-era crude prices — the June print (due mid-July) should moderate sharply given Brent has fallen from conflict highs to ~$83/bbl.

items.title: India's CPI inflation rises to 3.93% in May 2026; food-led but below RBI 4% target
items.summary: CPI rose to 3.93% in May from 3.48% in April, driven by food inflation at 4.78%. Core inflation firmed to 3.8%. Below Reuters poll forecast of 4.0% and under RBI's 4% midpoint target.
items.rationale: Below-target CPI combined with falling crude creates conditions for an August 2026 rate cut. The path from 3.93% in May toward the RBI's revised 5.1% FY27 projection has narrowed materially since oil fell from $124/bbl.

items.title: RBI MPC June 2026: Repo rate held at 5.25% for third meeting; FY27 CPI revised to 5.1%, GDP to 6.6%
items.summary: RBI MPC unanimously voted to hold repo rate at 5.25% with neutral stance. RBI revised FY27 GDP forecast down to 6.6% from 6.9%, and raised CPI inflation projection to 5.1% from 4.6%, citing West Asia conflict energy risks. RBI also eased FCNR(B) deposit norms — CRR/SLR exemption and full hedging cost borne by RBI for 3-5 year FCNR(B) deposits raised by September 30, 2026.
items.rationale: The hold removes near-term cut expectations but crude crash post-peace deal has already altered the inflation outlook. August MPC is now a live cut meeting. FCNR(B) measures support the rupee and attract NRI capital.

items.title: India's GDP grows 7.7% in FY26 — fastest in three years; Q4 at 7.8%, manufacturing at 10.7%
items.summary: MoSPI provisional estimates show India's FY26 real GDP grew 7.7%, beating consensus. Q4 FY26 clocked 7.8%. Manufacturing +10.7%; financial services +10.4%; trade/hotels/transport +11%. India Inc Q4 aggregate net profit grew 25.3% YoY — highest in nine quarters.
items.rationale: Strong FY26 GDP + corporate earnings record validate India's growth premium valuation and sustain FY27 earnings upgrade cycle, particularly for domestic cyclicals.

items.title: Nifty IT tumbles over 2%; HCL Tech -3.5%, Infosys -2.5%, TCS -1.7% on AI disruption fears
items.summary: Nifty IT index fell over 2% on June 11; HCL Tech -3.5%, Infosys -2.5%, TCS -1.7%. Anthropic's Fable5 model and TCS Chairman's comment that AI agents could equal human headcount by 2028 intensified fears of ADM revenue deflation. Nifty IT has fallen ~27% over the past six months.
items.rationale: Structural AI-led deflation of application development and maintenance (ADM) revenues is repricing the India IT sector. HCLTech's Sarvam AI acquisition (June 16) is a counter-move. Recovery likely requires Q1 FY27 results (July 2026) to show AI deal accretion inflecting positively.

items.title: First 'Made in India' Airbus C295 military transport aircraft completes maiden test flight from Vadodara
items.summary: India's first domestically manufactured Airbus C295 military transport aircraft successfully completed its maiden test flight from the Tata-Airbus Final Assembly Line in Vadodara, Gujarat. This is the first C295 of 40 to be built in India under a ₹21,935 crore deal. The IAF Deputy Chief of Air Staff witnessed the flight.
items.rationale: A milestone for India's private-sector defence manufacturing under Make in India. Proves India's aerospace execution capacity and provides template for future indigenous military aircraft programmes (AMCA, TEJAS Mk2). Medium-term positive for the entire defence aerospace supply chain.

items.title: Defence Ministry signs ₹1,476 crore deal with BEL for mobile electronic warfare systems
items.summary: The Defence Ministry signed a ₹1,476 crore contract with BEL for five ground-based mobile electronic warfare systems for the Indian Army under Buy (Indian-IDDM) category with 72% minimum indigenous content.
items.rationale: Adds to BEL's order book with multi-year execution visibility. BEL has seen Revenue CAGR of 16.6% over FY23-26. Continued order accretion from the ₹2.3-2.8 lakh crore defence pipeline supports earnings upgrades.

items.title: Godrej Properties sells homes worth ₹2,000 crore at Bengaluru Godrej Vanantara in launch week
items.summary: Godrej Properties sold 1,000+ homes worth ₹2,000 crore at Godrej Vanantara, Bengaluru, in launch week. The 36-acre project has total estimated revenue potential of ₹3,700 crore. GPL retained India's largest residential developer by booking value for the third consecutive year in FY26, with bookings +16% YoY to ₹34,171 crore.
items.rationale: Sustained demand for premium residential launches validates the structural realty upcycle. GPL's booking run rate of ₹34,171 crore in FY26 with 3-year CAGR of 41% positions it for further re-rating as rate cut expectations firm up.

items.title: Nifty Realty surges ~4% on June 15 — best-performing sector; DLF +4.67%, Prestige +6.14%
items.summary: Nifty Realty was the top-performing sector on June 15 (+~4%) as falling Brent crude improved inflation outlook and raised rate-cut probability. DLF (+4.67%), Godrej Properties (+4.42%), Prestige Estates (+6.14%), Oberoi Realty, and Phoenix Mills led gains.
items.rationale: Rate cut expectations, structural housing demand upcycle, and the peace deal macro tailwind create a powerful near-term catalyst for realty. Rising affordability (lower EMIs from rate cuts) is a key demand driver for residential real estate.

items.title: Wipro ₹15,000 crore share buyback closes June 17; settlement by June 24 at ₹250/share
items.summary: Wipro's ₹15,000 crore tender-offer buyback at ₹250/share (5.72% of equity, 60 crore shares) closes June 17, with settlement by June 24 and share extinguishment by July 6. Record date was June 5, 2026.
items.rationale: Buyback price provides near-term floor; small shareholders benefit from exit opportunity. Does not address structural AI revenue deflation risk. Watch for Q1 FY27 guidance for longer-term direction.

items.title: India's IIP grows 4.9% in April 2026 under new base year; capital goods surge 16%
items.summary: India's factory output grew 4.9% YoY in April 2026 under the revised IIP series. Manufacturing +6.2%; capital goods +16% YoY. Consumer durables +4.3% and consumer non-durables only +2.8% — uneven recovery.
items.rationale: Capital goods' 16% surge is strongly positive for infrastructure and capex plays. The lagging consumer recovery suggests FMCG and consumer discretionary face near-term volume pressure.

items.title: RBI FCNR(B) CRR/SLR exemption and full hedging cost swap for 3-5 year deposits through September 2026
items.summary: RBI opened an FCNR(B) swap window: fresh 3-5 year FCNR(B) dollar deposits mobilised by banks between June 8 and September 30, 2026 are exempt from CRR and SLR. RBI also bears the full hedging cost (~2.5% p.a.) on such deposits, making effective NRI deposit rates over 5.5% on USD. Smaller banks are offering over 7% on USD deposits.
items.rationale: The FCNR(B) measures are designed to attract NRI/foreign capital into India's banking system, supporting the rupee and foreign exchange reserves. Banks benefit from a stable, low-cost deposit window.

items.title: India Inc Q4 FY26 earnings: Aggregate net profit +25.3% YoY — highest in 9 quarters; revenue +10.8%
items.summary: India Inc aggregate Q4 FY26 net profit grew 25.3% YoY across 4,455+ companies — the highest in at least nine quarters. Revenue grew 10.8%. Nifty 50 net profit grew 2.0% (lagging due to IT and banking NIM compression). Cyclical sectors — oil, metals, power, and NBFCs — led the earnings surge.
items.rationale: Broad-based corporate earnings strength entering FY27 provides a fundamental underpinning for valuations. Cyclicals and mid/small-caps outperforming large-cap suggests wider earnings distribution — a hallmark of mature bull cycles.

items.title: Macquarie sees power sector upcycle; NTPC target ₹480 amid demand boom from data centres, EVs
items.summary: Macquarie upgraded India's power sector view, preferring NTPC (TP ₹480) and JSW Energy, citing a sustained electricity demand boom from industrialisation, data centres, EV adoption, and cooling load expansion.
items.rationale: India's power demand growth creates a multi-year capacity expansion cycle independent of crude oil dynamics. NTPC's renewables diversification and strong order book support the thesis.

items.title: L&T signs ₹18,600 crore MoU with Tamil Nadu for data centres, electronics, and shipbuilding
items.summary: L&T signed an ₹18,600 crore MoU with Tamil Nadu government covering three sectors: data centres, electronics manufacturing, and shipbuilding. The projects will generate 8,200 jobs.
items.rationale: L&T's multi-sector TN MoU diversifies revenue into high-margin digital infrastructure (data centres) while leveraging the national shipbuilding push. L&T was among top Nifty gainers on June 15 (+4.85%).

items.title: Indian steelmakers to launch multi-year capex cycle; 300 MT target; safeguard duties to April 2028 — S&P Global
items.summary: S&P Global reports Indian steelmakers are beginning a multi-year capex cycle to reach 300 MT capacity. Safeguard duties of 11-12% on steel imports extended through April 2028, protecting domestic producers from cheaper overseas supply.
items.rationale: Extended safeguard duties plus long-term domestic capacity expansion create a supportive multi-year cycle for steel producers. Near-term WPI-driven input cost pressure is a headwind but should ease with crude correction.

items.title: India-US trade framework: 99% issues resolved; Section 301 tariff public hearings set for July 7
items.summary: India and US reaffirmed BTA commitment after June 1-4 talks in New Delhi. US Ambassador Gor said 99% of issues resolved. Separately, USTR proposed Section 301 tariffs (12.5%) on India and 59 other countries; public hearings July 7. USTR Greer visits June 22 for final-stage talks.
items.rationale: BTA finalisation (expected by mid-July) would remove the Section 301 tariff threat for Indian IT and pharma exporters, directly reducing regulatory risk premium and potentially triggering FII buying in export sectors.

items.title: DIIs absorb ₹4.16 lakh crore YTD in 2026; monthly SIPs at ₹31,115 crore in April — record structural support
items.summary: Domestic institutions net bought ₹4.16 lakh crore between January and early June 2026, far exceeding FII outflows of ₹2.7 lakh crore YTD. Monthly SIP inflows at ₹31,115 crore in April (+18% YoY). DII holdings at 18.7% of NSE-listed equities — a record high.
items.rationale: India's equity market is increasingly self-funded through domestic savings channels. This structural shift reduces FII selling impact and provides a sustained demand floor, fundamentally altering India's market risk profile versus historical periods.

items.title: India's equity mutual fund inflows fall to 1-year low at ₹22,908 crore in May amid war volatility
items.summary: Equity mutual fund inflows fell 40% MoM to ₹22,908 crore in May 2026 — 1-year low — as West Asia war-driven market volatility dampened sentiment. Small/mid/large-cap fund inflows all declined. SIP flows held steady.
items.rationale: One-year low in equity MF inflows signals reduced risk appetite during the conflict period. With the peace deal now in place, June flows are expected to recover sharply. The SIP floor remained intact at ₹31,115 crore.

items.title: India's fiscal deficit FY26 at 4.4% of GDP — met target; FY27 April deficit already at 21.4% of annual target
items.summary: India's FY26 provisional fiscal deficit: 4.4% of GDP, meeting the revised estimate. FY27 April deficit: 21.4% of annual target as revenue receipts fell sharply. Government's FY27 target remains 4.4% of GDP.
items.rationale: Fiscal discipline in FY26 is positive for sovereign rating trajectory and bond market. April FY27 overshoot is seasonal; recovery in Q2-Q3 tax revenue collections will determine the full-year fiscal path.

items.title: NLC India shares fall 3% on government OFS of 3% stake for ~₹1,263 crore
items.summary: NLC India shares fell 3% to ₹327 after the government announced an OFS of up to 3% stake, aiming to raise ~₹1,263 crore. Part of the government's FY27 divestment programme.
items.rationale: OFS creates near-term supply overhang and price pressure. NLC India's relative underperformance vs broader market on OFS day signals institutional resistance to absorb at market prices.

items.title: India's monsoon running 28% below normal as of June 15; advance stalled — IMD weather briefing
items.summary: India's cumulative rainfall as of June 15, 2026 is 28% below the Long Period Average. Monsoon advancement has stalled at the Western Ghats; 16 states are still waiting for monsoon onset. A monsoon 'break' is expected June 16-21 with further reduction in activity on the Western Coast. Central India, northwest India face continued delay.
items.rationale: An early-season deficit of 28% below LPA, combined with the IMD's seasonal forecast of 90% of LPA, signals material risk to the Kharif crop, food inflation, and rural consumption. FMCG, two-wheeler, and agri-NBFC names are most vulnerable if deficit persists.

items.title: HDFC Bank hikes MCLR by up to 10 bps from June 8; NIM support amid elevated cost environment
items.summary: HDFC Bank hiked its MCLR by up to 10 basis points across tenors effective June 8, 2026. Base rate, BPLR, and FD rates unchanged. The hike raises yields on floating rate loans.
items.rationale: MCLR hike is directionally supportive of NIM on floating-rate book. However, the pending RBI rate cut cycle (August MPC is live) and falling 10Y G-sec yield mean the repricing window may be short. Watch for HDFC Bank's Q1 FY27 NIM trajectory.
